original_abstract: Tewang Kadamba Village, located in Katingan Regency, Central Kalimantan, is a diverse community characterized by a variety of ethnic groups and the practice of three primary religions: Islam, Hinduism (Kaharingan), and Christianity. While the village maintains a commendable level of interfaith harmony and mutual respect, there are noticeable challenges regarding the depth of religious knowledge and the overall enthusiasm for religious practice among its residents. This study explores the mechanisms of religious understanding within the village and evaluates strategies for fostering spiritual growth. Utilizing the Participatory Action Research (PAR) method, the initiative focused on identifying how residents acquire religious literacy and implementing empowerment strategies through the systematic regeneration of community leaders. The results indicate that while formal schooling and house of worship activities remain the primary conduits for religious education, there is a critical need for structured cadre development. The findings suggest that optimizing religious strengthening through organized regeneration is a viable and effective model for community empowerment in Tewang Kadamba, ensuring the sustainability of religious knowledge and practice for future generations.
humanized_abstract: A survey was conducted in Tewang Kadamba Village, one of the villages in Katingan Regency, Central Kalimantan. The village is inhabited by various ethnic groups with three major religions practiced, namely Islam, Hinduism (Kaharingan) and Christian. Despite the mutual respect between people of different religions, this village still has many problems such as lacking deep understanding of their religion and lack of enthusiasm in the religous practices. This study discussed the systems of religious understanding at the village level, and the ways in which to strengthen the religious practises by cadre regeneration through an action research (AR) using Participatory Action Research (PAR) approaches. Although formal education and places of worship remain the main medium for giving religious education, the study however shows the need of structured cadre development to enhance the religious strengthening activities of the community. The research also found out that strengthening religion in the village using approaches of cadre regeneration activity is a very effective and systematic approach in order to carry out community empowerment activities, so that in the future the community will still carry out their religious practices and knowing the religious doctrines appropriately.
